title: enarthrocarpus strangulatus var. anceps
intro: Enarthrocarpus strangulatus var. anceps, also known as the strangling vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Santalaceae, native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits of E. strangulatus var. anceps include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and acting as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety.
